checkpoint_context.active_numbering_note: Current checkpoint law advances EVENT_LOG_AND_REPLAY_DOCTRINE-0 as Φ-B0 after the post-Σ-B / post-Φ-A2 checkpoint even though older planning artifacts may still retain stale numbering drift.

replay_definition.event_log_summary: An event log is a declared, auditable, externalized or externalizable ordered record of replay-relevant transitions or evidence classes produced under explicit semantic, runtime, and governance law.
replay_definition.replay_summary: Replay is the lawful reconstruction, validation, reenactment, or dispute-oriented re-examination of runtime and truth-relevant history using authoritative event classes, explicit state law, deterministic ordering law, and declared continuity rules.

event_classes.description: Records lawful transitions relevant to authoritative truth evolution, Process execution, or deterministic state progression.
Records authority posture, entitlement, capability, ownership-sensitive refusal, or legality-relevant gate outcomes.
Records transitions whose legality depends on explicit cross-domain bridge law or bridge-aware routing.
Records runtime lifecycle posture changes such as activate, suspend, degrade, fail, stop, or recover.
Records binding attach, detach, replay-anchor attachment, mediation posture changes, or other binding-law-relevant shifts.
Records lineage, revision, formalization stage, protocol posture, or institutional state needed to interpret history lawfully.
Records view, observation, rendered, or presentation-local history without changing authoritative truth.
Records operator-visible refusal, degradation, checkpoint-trigger, or replay-control state that may or may not affect authoritative replay.
Records instrumentation, debugging, or performance-oriented history whose primary role is observability.

distinction_notes.authoritative_replay_event: A replay-relevant event class that may be used to reconstruct, validate, or contest lawful truth and runtime legality over time.
distinction_notes.auxiliary_event: A useful evidence class for investigation or reenactment that is not authoritative by default.
distinction_notes.snapshot: An immutable state anchor or view consumed under state law, not the same thing as ordered replay history.
distinction_notes.save_persistence_artifact: An externalized state artifact distinct from replay semantics.
distinction_notes.projected_generated_state: Operational evidence or convenience output that may not stand in for canonical replay source by default.
distinction_notes.control_plane_log: An operator-visible or policy-visible record that is replay-relevant only when it alters lawful runtime posture or truth-affecting execution legality.
distinction_notes.ui_history: Presentation-local history that remains non-authoritative unless explicitly promoted under stronger law.

replay_failure_invalidity_categories.description: Required replay-relevant events are absent or incomplete.
Event structure cannot be parsed or validated lawfully.
Deterministic ordering, causal ordering, or declared sequencing is broken.
Replay equivalence or deterministic validation fails.
History implies authority or ownership transitions not supported by ownership law.
Cross-domain effects appear without lawful bridge basis.
Lifecycle transitions are missing, contradictory, or unlawful.
Only projected or generated artifacts remain where canonical replay source is required.
Lineage or formalization context needed for lawful interpretation is missing.
Event meaning depends on absent or incompatible contract, capability, or version context.
Replay is possible only for bounded questions and may not claim full authority.
Multiple incompatible histories or evidentiary claims remain unresolved.

ownership_projection_cautions: fields/ remains canonical semantic field substrate while field/ remains transitional
schema/ remains canonical semantic contract law while schemas/ remains a projection or validator-facing mirror
packs/ remains canonical for runtime packaging and activation scope while data/packs/ remains scoped authored-pack authority
canonical prose and semantic specs outrank projected or generated mirrors
the thin runtime/ root is not canonical by name alone
older planning numbering drift remains evidence rather than authority
downstream_refinement_prompts: later Ζ continuity, migration, restartless, and live-ops gating
